{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 山田上ノ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
墳丘正面
（[[孝徳天皇]]大阪磯長陵 拝所） |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字山田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 3.14 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 39|経度秒 = 15.84 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径32m |築造年代 = |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第36代[[孝徳天皇]] |出土品 = |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「大阪磯長陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 山田上ノ山古墳 |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|30|58|N|135|38|43|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 320| height =250 | caption = {{center|[[磯長谷古墳群]]分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|30|42.60|N|135|38|7.75|E}} | label1=太子西山 (敏達) | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-北西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|31|0.43|N|135|38|39.13|E}} | label2=春日向山 (用明) | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|30|42.83|N|135|38|58.44|E}} | label3=山田高塚 (推古) | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|31|3.14|N|135|39|15.84|E}} | label4=山田上ノ山 (孝徳) | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|31|11.23|N|135|38|21.38|E}} | label5=叡福寺北 (聖徳太子) | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|30|38.96|N|135|39|5.65|E}} | label6=二子塚 | label-pos6=bottom | label-color6=black | mark6=方墳.png | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|30|41.09|N|135|38|32.64|E}} | label7=釜戸塚 | label-pos7=top | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=30 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|34|30|38.94|N|135|38|33.84|E}} | label8=石塚 | label-pos8=bottom | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=30 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|34|30|40.17|N|135|38|36.33|E}} | label9=葉室塚 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=方墳.png | mark-size9=30 }} '''山田上ノ山古墳'''（やまだうえのやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大阪磯長陵'''（おおさかのしながのみささぎ）」として第36代[[孝徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、二上山から西方に派生する丘陵の先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。丘陵前には[[竹内街道]]が通る{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。[[江戸時代]]には「北山陵」と称されたほか{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}、かつては「鶯陵（うぐいすのみささぎ）」とも称され{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html 孝徳天皇陵（山田上ノ山古墳）]（太子町ホームページ）。、『河内名所図会』では『[[枕草子]]』の「うぐひすのみささぎ」を本古墳に比定する{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}『枕草子』の「うぐひすのみささぎ」については、[[鶯塚古墳]]（奈良県奈良市）に比定する説もある（「鶯塚古墳」『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年）。。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は円形で、南西方に面する{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。墳丘は直径32メートルを測り、小規模な円墳になる{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。埋葬施設などは明らかでない{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。関連出土品として、かつて[[陪塚]]から出土したという[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が知られるが、その出土地の詳細は明らかでない（現在では陪塚等は知られない）{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在は[[宮内庁]]により第36代[[孝徳天皇]]（[[654年]]崩御）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。[[7世紀]]の王陵としては懐疑的な見解もある一方『新編日本古典文学全集 4 日本書紀 (3)』小学館、2003年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、p. 199。、その小さい墳丘規模に生前の薄葬令の反映を推測する説もある{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。磯長谷では孝徳天皇陵のほか[[敏達天皇|敏達]]・[[用明天皇|用明]]・[[推古天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される。 == 被葬者 == 山田上ノ山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第36代'''[[孝徳天皇]]'''の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/12}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）12コマ。{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。孝徳天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では[[白雉]]5年（[[654年]]）10月『日本書紀』白雉5年（654年）10月壬子（10日）条。に崩御し、同年12月『日本書紀』白雉5年（654年）12月己酉（8日）条。に「大坂磯長陵」に葬られたとする{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では孝徳天皇陵は遠陵の「大坂磯長陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西5町・南北5町で守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。その後、元禄の探陵の際に現陵に治定され（他に古市にも孝徳天皇陵伝承地が存在した）、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）に修復および拝所整備等が実施された{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。現在の陵号には「坂」でなく「阪」の字が使用される。 {{Supra|37}} [[斉明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|斉明天皇}}]]|03={{Supra|34}} [[舒明天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[関晃]] 「孝徳天皇」|ref={{Harvid|孝徳天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「大阪磯長陵」（孝徳天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=上ノ山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=孝徳天皇陵|title=王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群 -太子町の古墳墓-|publisher=太子町教育委員会（[[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]]）|pages=21-22|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[孝徳天皇]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html 河内磯長中尾陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html 孝徳天皇陵（山田上ノ山古墳）] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:やまたうえのやまこふん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:孝徳天皇]]
[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:new pages]]

[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:new pages]] {{Ancient Japanese Burial Mound | Name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

revision.text:
[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] {{Infobox ancient site | Name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] {{Infobox ancient site | Name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:images]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

#REDIRECT [[Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]]
[[Category:mass bump]]

[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] {{Infobox ancient site | Name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]
