According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

[[Category:dd]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinga Valley Burial Mounds]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]]

In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == ({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == ({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == ({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == ({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takeuchi Highway|ja|竹内街道}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage
