Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * revision.text:
[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinga Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654年}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | Label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinga revision.comment: attempt at mass correcting bad parameters on indofox ancient site
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | Excavated items = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinga Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | Alternate name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | Latitude = 34.5175 | Longitude = 135.6544 | ISO = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | Burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | Icon = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin Burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} *
