({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has other_designation1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has other_designation1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has other_designation1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinga Valley Kofuns]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == ({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == ({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 32m | Construction year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year }}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Imperial Tombs." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Tombs] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 山田上ノ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
墳丘正面
（[[孝徳天皇]]大阪磯長陵 拝所） |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字山田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 3.14 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 39|経度秒 = 15.84 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径32m |築造年代 = |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第36代[[孝徳天皇]] |出土品 = |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「大阪磯長陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 山田上ノ山古墳 |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|30|58|N|135|38|43|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 320| height =250 | caption = {{center|[[磯長谷古墳群]]分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|30|42.60|N|135|38|7.75|E}} | label1=太子西山 (敏達) | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-北西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|31|0.43|N|135|38|39.13|E}} | label2=春日向山 (用明) | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|30|42.83|N|135|38|58.44|E}} | label3=山田高塚 (推古) | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|31|3.14|N|135|39|15.84|E}} | label4=山田上ノ山 (孝徳) | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|31|11.23|N|135|38|21.38|E}} | label5=叡福寺北 (聖徳太子) | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|30|38.96|N|135|39|5.65|E}} | label6=二子塚 | label-pos6=bottom | label-color6=black | mark6=方墳.png | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|30|41.09|N|135|38|32.64|E}} | label7=釜戸塚 | label-pos7=top | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=30 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|34|30|38.94|N|135|38|33.84|E}} | label8=石塚 | label-pos8=bottom | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=30 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|34|30|40.17|N|135|38|36.33|E}} | label9=葉室塚 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=方墳.png | mark-size9=30 }} '''山田上ノ山古墳'''（やまだうえのやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大阪磯長陵'''（おおさかのしながのみささぎ）」として第36代[[孝徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、二上山から西方に派生する丘陵の先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。丘陵前には[[竹内街道]]が通る{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。[[江戸時代]]には「北山陵」と称されたほか{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}、かつては「鶯陵（うぐいすのみささぎ）」とも称され{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html 孝徳天皇陵（山田上ノ山古墳）]（太子町ホームページ）。、『河内名所図会』では『[[枕草子]]』の「うぐひすのみささぎ」を本古墳に比定する{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}『枕草子』の「うぐひすのみささぎ」については、[[鶯塚古墳]]（奈良県奈良市）に比定する説もある（「鶯塚古墳」『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年）。。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は円形で、南西方に面する{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。墳丘は直径32メートルを測り、小規模な円墳になる{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。埋葬施設などは明らかでない{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。関連出土品として、かつて[[陪塚]]から出土したという[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が知られるが、その出土地の詳細は明らかでない（現在では陪塚等は知られない）{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在は[[宮内庁]]により第36代[[孝徳天皇]]（[[654年]]崩御）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。[[7世紀]]の王陵としては懐疑的な見解もある一方『新編日本古典文学全集 4 日本書紀 (3)』小学館、2003年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、p. 199。、その小さい墳丘規模に生前の薄葬令の反映を推測する説もある{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。磯長谷では孝徳天皇陵のほか[[敏達天皇|敏達]]・[[用明天皇|用明]]・[[推古天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される。 == 被葬者 == 山田上ノ山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第36代'''[[孝徳天皇]]'''の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/12}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）12コマ。{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。孝徳天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では[[白雉]]5年（[[654年]]）10月『日本書紀』白雉5年（654年）10月壬子（10日）条。に崩御し、同年12月『日本書紀』白雉5年（654年）12月己酉（8日）条。に「大坂磯長陵」に葬られたとする{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では孝徳天皇陵は遠陵の「大坂磯長陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西5町・南北5町で守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。その後、元禄の探陵の際に現陵に治定され（他に古市にも孝徳天皇陵伝承地が存在した）、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）に修復および拝所整備等が実施された{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。現在の陵号には「坂」でなく「阪」の字が使用される。 {{Supra|37}} [[斉明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|斉明天皇}}]]|03={{Supra|34}} [[舒明天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[関晃]] 「孝徳天皇」|ref={{Harvid|孝徳天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「大阪磯長陵」（孝徳天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=上ノ山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=孝徳天皇陵|title=王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群 -太子町の古墳墓-|publisher=太子町教育委員会（[[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]]）|pages=21-22|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[孝徳天皇]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html 河内磯長中尾陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html 孝徳天皇陵（山田上ノ山古墳）] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:やまたうえのやまこふん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:孝徳天皇]]

mark-size9=30 }} '''山田上ノ山古墳'''（やまだうえのやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大阪磯長陵'''（おおさかのしながのみささぎ）」として第36代[[孝徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、二上山から西方に派生する丘陵の先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。丘陵前には[[竹内街道]]が通る{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。[[江戸時代]]には「北山陵」と称されたほか{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}、かつては「鶯陵（うぐいすのみささぎ）」とも称され{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html 孝徳天皇陵（山田上ノ山古墳）]（太子町ホームページ）。、『河内名所図会』では『[[枕草子]]』の「うぐひすのみささぎ」を本古墳に比定する{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}『枕草子』の「うぐひすのみささぎ」については、[[鶯塚古墳]]（奈良県奈良市）に比定する説もある（「鶯塚古墳」『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年）。。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は円形で、南西方に面する{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。墳丘は直径32メートルを測り、小規模な円墳になる{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。埋葬施設などは明らかでない{{Sfn|上ノ山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。関連出土品として、かつて[[陪塚]]から出土したという[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が知られるが、その出土地の詳細は明らかでない（現在では陪塚等は知られない）{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在は[[宮内庁]]により第36代[[孝徳天皇]]（[[654年]]崩御）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。[[7世紀]]の王陵としては懐疑的な見解もある一方『新編日本古典文学全集 4 日本書紀 (3)』小学館、2003年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、p. 199。、その小さい墳丘規模に生前の薄葬令の反映を推測する説もある{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=21-22}}。磯長谷では孝徳天皇陵のほか[[敏達天皇|敏達]]・[[用明天皇|用明]]・[[推古天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される。 == 被葬者 == 山田上ノ山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第36代'''[[孝徳天皇]]'''の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/12}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）12コマ。{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。孝徳天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では[[白雉]]5年（[[654年]]）10月『日本書紀』白雉5年（654年）10月壬子（10日）条。に崩御し、同年12月『日本書紀』白雉5年（654年）12月己酉（8日）条。に「大坂磯長陵」に葬られたとする{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では孝徳天皇陵は遠陵の「大坂磯長陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西5町・南北5町で守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。その後、元禄の探陵の際に現陵に治定され（他に古市にも孝徳天皇陵伝承地が存在した）、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）に修復および拝所整備等が実施された{{Sfn|大阪磯長陵（国史）}}。現在の陵号には「坂」でなく「阪」の字が使用される。 This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter: 32m | built year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter: 32m | built year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]]

({{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter: 32m | built year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavations = | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]]

The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku|qq=}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Pillow Book|qq=}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Pillow Book|qq=}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uguisuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku|qq=}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/654|qq=}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bidatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōmei|qq=}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko|qq=}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku|qq=}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku era|qq=}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1864|qq=}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]]

Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Taishi, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Pillow Book|qq=}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Pillow Book|qq=}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uguisuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamikawachi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku|qq=}}. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Pillow Book|qq=}}.Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in {{ill|The Pillow Book|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Pillow Book|qq=}} to {{ill|Uguisuzuka Kofun|ja|鶯塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uguisuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}} (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}, and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku|qq=}} (who passed away in {{ill|654|ja|654th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/654|qq=}}). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=}}.{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors {{ill|Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bidatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōmei|qq=}}, {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko|qq=}}, and Prince {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=}}, collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor {{ill|Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku|qq=}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}.{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}}, with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku era|qq=}} (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in {{ill|1864|ja|元治元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1864|qq=}}. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]]
