([[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 32m | built year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] | excavations = | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]]. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=}}.[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]]. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府太子の Municipal History]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}

Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=}}.[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]]. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Isonagatani Kofun Group]] {{Commons category|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
([[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 32m | built year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] | excavations = | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]]. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=}}.[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]]. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front view of the mound
([[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s Osaka Shinga Mausoleum) | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34.5175 | coordinates = 135.6544 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 32m | built year = Unknown | burial chamber = Unknown | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] | excavations = | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" | Features = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Yamada Uenoyama Kofun | type = Circular tumulus | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]]. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=}}.[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]]. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]]. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=}}.[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]]. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}} [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]]. == Overview == The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981). Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted. The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=}}.[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi). The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums." == Interred Individual == The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial. During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]]. == notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/036/index.html Osaka Shinga Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/kotokutennoryo.html Emperor Kōtoku Mausoleum (Yamada Uenoyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|version=93243025|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]][[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yamada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E7%94%B0%E4%B8%8A%E3%83%8E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:山田上ノ山古墳]] [[sn:amada Uenoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kōtoku]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun}}

'''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856365}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].

== Overview ==
The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).<ref name="Taishi">{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].<ref group="Note">Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981).</ref> Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted.

The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}}.<ref name="Kotoku">[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi).</ref>

The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums."

== Interred Individual ==
The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].<ref>[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].</ref><ref>{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934).</ref> According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year.<ref name="Kotoku"/> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.<ref name="Engishiki">[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial.</ref> During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]].

'''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856365}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].

== Overview ==
The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).<ref name="Taishi">{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].<ref group="Note">Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981).</ref> Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted.

The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|qq=|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}}.<ref name="Kotoku">[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi).</ref>

The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums."

== Interred Individual ==
The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].<ref>[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].</ref><ref>{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934).</ref> According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year.<ref name="Kotoku"/> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.<ref name="Engishiki">[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial.</ref> During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]].

'''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856365}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].

== Overview ==
The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).<ref name="Taishi">{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].<ref group="Note">Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981).</ref> Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted.

The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}}.<ref name="Kotoku">[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi).</ref>

The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums."

== Interred Individual ==
The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].<ref>[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].</ref><ref>{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934).</ref> According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year.<ref name="Kotoku"/> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.<ref name="Engishiki">[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial.</ref> During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]].

'''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856365}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].

== Overview ==
The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).<ref name="Taishi">{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].<ref group="Note">Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981).</ref> Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted.

The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}}.<ref name="Kotoku">[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi).</ref>

The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums."

== Interred Individual ==
The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].<ref>[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].</ref><ref>{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934).</ref> According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year.<ref name="Kotoku"/> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.<ref name="Engishiki">[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial.</ref> During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]].

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}}.<ref name="Kotoku">[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi).</ref>

The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums."

== Interred Individual ==
The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].<ref>[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].</ref><ref>{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934).</ref> According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year.<ref name="Kotoku"/> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.<ref name="Engishiki">[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial.</ref> During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]].

'''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856365}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].

== Overview ==
The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).<ref name="Taishi">{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].<ref group="Note">Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981).</ref> Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted.

The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}}.<ref name="Kotoku">[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi).</ref>

The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums."

== Interred Individual ==
The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].<ref>[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].</ref><ref>{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934).</ref> According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year.<ref name="Kotoku"/> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.<ref name="Engishiki">[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial.</ref> During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]].

'''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856365}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].

== Overview ==
The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).<ref name="Taishi">{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].<ref group="Note">Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981).</ref> Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted.

The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}}.<ref name="Kotoku">[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi).</ref>

The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums."

== Interred Individual ==
The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].<ref>[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].</ref><ref>{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934).</ref> According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year.<ref name="Kotoku"/> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.<ref name="Engishiki">[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial.</ref> During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]].

'''Yamada Uenoyama Kofun''' (山田上ノ山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856365}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "'''Osaka Shinga Mausoleum'''" (大阪磯長陵), the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].

== Overview ==
The mound is located in southeastern Osaka Prefecture, at the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, where it was constructed on a hill extending westward from the mountain. In front of the hill lies the {{ill|Takenouchi Kaidō|ja|竹内街道|1=Takenouchi Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Kaidō|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was referred to as the "Kitayama Mausoleum" and was also known as the "Uguisu Mausoleum" (鶯陵).<ref name="Taishi">{{ill|Taishi Town Homepage|ja|太子町ホームページ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Town Homepage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> The {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} links this site to the "Uguisu Mausoleum" mentioned in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]].<ref group="Note">Some sources attribute the "Uguisu Mausoleum" in [[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]] to [[:en:Uguisuzuka Kofun|Uguisuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (see "Uguisuzuka Kofun," {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1981).</ref> Currently, the tomb is under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], and no detailed investigations have been conducted.

The tumulus is circular, with a diameter of 32 meters, and faces southwest. While details of the burial facilities are unclear, the site is known for the {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea-beast grape mirror|lt=Sea Beast Grape Mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea Beast Grape Mirror|qq=}} supposedly excavated from a surrounding satellite mound, though its exact location remains unknown. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]] (who passed away in [[:en:654|654]]). The modest size of the tumulus may reflect Emperor Kōtoku's adherence to the {{ill|Thin burial Decree|ja|薄葬令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thin burial Decree|1=Thin Burial Edict|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622174}}.<ref name="Kotoku">[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Hakuchi 5 (654), October 10 (Jinshi).</ref>

The Shinga Valley is home to other imperial tombs attributed to Emperors [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Bidatsu]], [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Yōmei]], [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]], and Prince [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku]], collectively referred to as the "Umebachi Mausoleums."

== Interred Individual ==
The actual individual interred at Yamada Uenoyama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of the 36th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Kōtoku]].<ref>[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]].</ref><ref>{{NDLDC|1237123/12|title=Ryo Bo Yoran}} (Imperial Household Ministry, 1934).</ref> According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku passed away in the 10th month of Hakuchi 5 (654) and was buried at "Osaka Shinga Mausoleum" in the 12th month of the same year.<ref name="Kotoku"/> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], compiled in the 10th century, describes this mausoleum as a "remote tomb" in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], with a precinct of 5 chō (approximately 545 meters) in both east-west and north-south directions, and three caretakers annually assigned to its maintenance.<ref name="Engishiki">[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], vol. 21, Ministry of Ceremonial.</ref> During the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1699), the tomb was officially identified as Emperor Kōtoku's mausoleum, and renovations were carried out in [[:en:1864|1864]].
